regime_call.reasoning: Composite scores oscillate near zero (defensive_rotation +9.8, risk_on_off +8.6 both MIXED) but internals show clear domestic-to-international capital rotation with sector bifurcation (materials IN, tech/financials OUT). No single composite exceeds |20|, but flow extremes (EWU +5.13σ, UNG -3.97σ, IBIT -2.54σ) suggest transition underway.
regime_call.what_would_invalidate: Reversal in country ETF flows (EWU/EWC turning negative) or QQQ 5-day flow flipping positive with >+1σ would signal domestic re-risk

ai_output.regime_alpha_note: Current NEUTRAL regime with DOMESTIC_OUTFLOW internals argues for de-rating all research STRONG_BUY calls on US mega-cap growth (MSFT, GOOGL, CRM, ORCL) from HIGH to MEDIUM conviction until international rotation exhausts (EWU/EWC z-scores normalize below +2.0). Conversely, any HOLD verdicts on international equities or commodity plays (LIN, materials names) deserve upgrade to BUY given flow alignment. Research coverage bias (100% US, 0% international) creates structural alpha opportunity in EWU/EWC/VEA until analyst community catches up to capital flows.

self_assessment.biggest_unknown: Insider transaction data completely absent (all null) — cluster buying/selling at sector level (XLK, XLF, XLB) would significantly refine conviction on AAPL/QQQ/XLB calls and validate or contradict flow signals

ai_output.narrative_preview: Today's dominant signal is the violent international rotation: EWU +5.13σ (UK), EWC +3.18σ (Canada), and EWG +1.28σ (Germany) represent extreme smart-money flight from US exposure. Domestic large-cap indices (SPY, QQQ, VTI) bleed -$5.4B while international country ETFs absorb +$316M with 1-day persistence across developed markets. This is paired with aggressive sector divergence — XLB (materials) surging +2.7σ while XLK (tech) bleeds -1.68σ and XLF (financials) -1.11σ. The cross-asset story: ris
